summary: Converted sysdolphin baselib archive code from assembly into C/header form enough to build archive.o from src, matching some HSD archive helpers while leaving HSD_ArchiveParse and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern guarded as NON_MATCHING/inline assembly due to unresolved matching issues. The PR also renamed the MSL string symbol func_803258E8 to strcmp and updated callers/prototypes so archive code could use a real string comparison API.
what_changed: Renamed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.s to src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.c and replaced portions of raw assembly with C implementations.

Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.h defining HSD_ArchiveHeader, HSD_ArchiveRelocationInfo, HSD_ArchivePublicInfo, HSD_ArchiveExternInfo, HSD_Archive, HSD_ARCHIVE_DONT_FREE, and prototypes for archive APIs.
Implemented a C inline Locate helper that applies relocation entries by adding archive->data to each relocated word.
Provided a C version of HSD_ArchiveParse under NON_MATCHING, while preserving the original assembly implementation otherwise.
Implemented matching C versions of HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress and HSD_ArchiveGetExtern, with #pragma peephole enabled around them.
Provided a C version of HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern under NON_MATCHING, while preserving the original assembly implementation otherwise.
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.o.
Added archive.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Renamed MSL string assembly label func_803258E8 to strcmp and updated assembly call sites in mcc, melee lb code, sysdolphin class, and archive inline asm.
Updated include/functions.h from an unknown func_803258E8 declaration to int strcmp(char*, char*).
Added a memcpy prototype to include/string.h.
smart_moves: Kept unresolved functions inside NON_MATCHING blocks and retained exact inline assembly fallbacks, allowing source migration without pretending fully matched code was ready.
Introduced archive.h at the same time as the C conversion, making field offsets explicit and reusable for future HSD archive decompilation work.
Used the recognized library symbol name strcmp instead of leaving func_803258E8 anonymous, then propagated that name to all known assembly call sites.
Moved only the build target for archive.o from asm to src after preserving assembly fallbacks in archive.c, minimizing integration risk.
Used #pragma peephole on around the simple matching helper functions, indicating compiler-codegen tuning was part of the match strategy.
decomp_lessons: When converting an asm file to C, it is acceptable to mix matched C functions with inline assembly fallbacks for functions that still fail to match.
Archive-like binary formats should be modeled with explicit structs and offset comments before attempting broader matching, because pointer arithmetic depends directly on field layout.
Library routine identification can unblock C conversion: naming func_803258E8 as strcmp enabled natural C implementations that call strcmp and made other call sites clearer.
Build-system changes are part of decomp progress: obj_files.mk must switch from asm object to src object only once the source file can still provide all required symbols.
Section placement/string-literal issues can prevent otherwise plausible C from matching; preserve asm when extern'ing strings or data splitting is not ready.
Use NON_MATCHING comments to record the concrete blocker so later contributors know whether the issue is codegen, data splitting, or an unknown logic mismatch.
naming_conventions: HSD archive APIs use the HSD_Archive prefix, e.g. HSD_ArchiveParse, HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, HSD_ArchiveGetExtern, and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern.
Archive data structs use HSD_Archive-prefixed typedefs with descriptive suffixes: Header, RelocationInfo, PublicInfo, ExternInfo.
Internal helper Locate is capitalized but not HSD-prefixed in this slice; evidence only shows it as an inline local helper in archive.c.
The old autogenerated/unknown symbol func_803258E8 was replaced with the standard library name strcmp.
The archive header include guard used lowercase underscore style: _archive_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline asm functions with nofralloc for HSD_ArchiveParse and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern when the C versions were still non-matching.
Wrapped non-matching C attempts in #ifdef NON_MATCHING so the source documents intended behavior without affecting the matching build path.
Enabled peephole optimization with #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress and HSD_ArchiveGetExtern.
Renamed assembly branch-link targets from func_803258E8 to strcmp without changing the underlying addresses or instruction bytes.
Added archive.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, suggesting this object required special scheduling/epilogue treatment for matching.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Author note: ArchiveParse is non-matching until data splitting is done because extern'ing the string makes the compiler throw a section error.
Author note: LocateExtern is non-matching due to an unresolved issue the author could not figure out.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove NON_MATCHING from HSD_ArchiveParse.
Find later PRs that resolve HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern matching.
Search for data splitting changes involving lbl_804074A8 or the HSD_ArchiveParse OSReport string.
Search for later cleanup of include/functions.h strcmp signature or movement into include/string.h.
Find PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.h field layouts.
